vocabulary.meaning: say sorry for having done something wrong
vocabulary.example: You should apologise to her for being late.

vocabulary.meaning: male friend with whom someone has a romantic relationship
vocabulary.example: She introduced her new boyfriend to her parents.

vocabulary.meaning: knowing someone very well and liking them very much
vocabulary.example: They have been close friends since childhood.

vocabulary.meaning: feeling sure about your own abilities or ideas
vocabulary.example: She is confident that she will pass the exam.

vocabulary.meaning: fashionable, attractive, or impressive
vocabulary.example: He looks really cool in his new leather jacket.

vocabulary.meaning: two people who are married or in a relationship
vocabulary.example: The young couple walked hand in hand along the beach.

vocabulary.meaning: make something look more attractive by putting things on it
vocabulary.example: We decorated the hall with balloons for the party.

vocabulary.meaning: protect someone or something from attack or harm
vocabulary.example: The soldiers fought bravely to defend their castle.

vocabulary.meaning: no longer married because the marriage has legally ended
vocabulary.example: Her parents got divorced when she was ten years old.

vocabulary.meaning: set of rooms for living in, usually on one floor of a building
vocabulary.example: They rented a small flat in the city center.

vocabulary.meaning: willing to give money, help, or gifts to others
vocabulary.example: It was very generous of him to pay for our dinner.

vocabulary.meaning: female friend with whom someone has a romantic relationship
vocabulary.example: He bought a beautiful necklace for his girlfriend.

vocabulary.meaning: feeling or showing thanks for something good
vocabulary.example: I am extremely grateful for your help.

vocabulary.meaning: person who is invited to visit someone's home or attend an event
vocabulary.example: We have several guests staying at our house this weekend.

vocabulary.meaning: not controlled or influenced by other people
vocabulary.example: She is an independent woman who lives on her own.

vocabulary.meaning: tell someone another person's name when they meet for the first time
vocabulary.example: Let me introduce you to my colleague, Sarah.

vocabulary.meaning: showing a lot of love and care for someone
vocabulary.example: He has a very warm and loving family.

vocabulary.meaning: remaining constant in your support of a friend or country
vocabulary.example: A dog is known as a loyal companion.

vocabulary.meaning: the way you are feeling at a particular time
vocabulary.example: She was in a good mood after receiving the good news.

vocabulary.meaning: area of a town or city where people live
vocabulary.example: This is a quiet and safe neighbourhood.

vocabulary.meaning: not special or different in any way; usual
vocabulary.example: It was just an ordinary day at work.

vocabulary.meaning: able to stay calm and wait without getting angry
vocabulary.example: You need to be patient when teaching children.

vocabulary.meaning: only for one person or group; not public
vocabulary.example: This is a private conversation; please leave us alone.

vocabulary.meaning: know someone or something because you have seen them before
vocabulary.example: I didn't recognise him at first because of his beard.

vocabulary.meaning: member of your family; connection between two things
vocabulary.example: Is he a relation of yours?

vocabulary.meaning: pay money regularly to live in a house; the money paid
vocabulary.example: They decided to rent a house near the beach.

vocabulary.meaning: admire someone because of their qualities or achievements; admiration
vocabulary.example: We should respect our teachers and elderly people.

vocabulary.meaning: not married or in a romantic relationship
vocabulary.example: He is single and enjoys travelling around the world.

vocabulary.meaning: person you do not know
vocabulary.example: Don't talk to strangers when you walk home alone.

vocabulary.meaning: believe that someone is good, honest, and reliable; belief in someone
vocabulary.example: Trust is very important in a friendship.

vocabulary.meaning: something that is kept hidden or known by only a few people
vocabulary.example: Can you keep a secret?

vocabulary.meaning: demanding that rules are obeyed and followed exactly
vocabulary.example: Our school has very strict rules about dress code.

vocabulary.meaning: state of being free to do what you want without control
vocabulary.example: Everyone values their freedom.

vocabulary.meaning: end a marriage or romantic relationship
vocabulary.example: They decided to split up after being together for three years.

vocabulary.meaning: form a picture or idea of something in your mind
vocabulary.example: Can you imagine what life will be like in fifty years?

phrasalVerbs.meaning: look after a child until they become an adult
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She brought up three children on her own.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: argue with someone and stop being friendly with them
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: He fell out with his best friend over a silly argument.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: have a good friendly relationship with someone
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I get on very well with my classmates.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: have a romantic relationship with someone
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She has been going out with him for six months.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: develop from being a child into an adult
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: He grew up in a small town in the countryside.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: disappoint someone by failing to do what they expected
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I promised to help her, and I won't let her down.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: take care of someone or something
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Could you look after my cat while I am on holiday?
EX: They decided to split up after a long discussion.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: alone; without help from anyone else
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: Did you build this bookshelf by yourself?

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: sharing interests, beliefs, or features with someone else
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: I don't have much in common with my brother.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: communicating or staying in touch with someone
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: We still keep in contact with our primary school friends.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: having strong romantic feelings for someone
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: They fell in love with each other during college.
EX: He broke the cup on purpose.
EX: She traveled to London on her own.

wordFormation.meaning: ability: power or skill to do something / disabled: having a physical or mental condition that limits movements / unable: not able to do something
wordFormation.example:
EX: She has the ability to speak three languages.
EX: The hotel provides facilities for disabled guests.
EX: I was unable to contact him yesterday.

wordFormation.meaning: admire: respect and approve of someone / admiration: feeling of respect and approval
wordFormation.example:
EX: I admire her courage and dedication.
EX: He looked at the painting with admiration.

wordFormation.meaning: careful: paying close attention to avoid danger or mistakes / careless: not paying enough attention; making mistakes
wordFormation.example:
EX: Please be careful when crossing the street.
EX: It was a careless mistake to leave the keys in the car.

wordFormation.meaning: confidence: feeling of trust or certainty in oneself or others
wordFormation.example: EX: Regular practice will build your confidence.

wordFormation.meaning: forgive / forgave / forgiven: stop feeling angry at someone for a mistake / forgiveness: act of forgiving someone
wordFormation.example:
EX: Please forgive me for forgetting your birthday.
EX: She asked for his forgiveness.

wordFormation.meaning: dishonest: not honest; telling lies or cheating / honesty: quality of being truthful and trustworthy
wordFormation.example:
EX: He was fired for being dishonest with customers.
EX: Honesty is the best policy.

wordFormation.meaning: introduction: act of presenting someone or something for the first time
wordFormation.example: EX: The book has a brief introduction to grammar.

wordFormation.meaning: liar: person who tells lies / lying: act of telling lies
wordFormation.example:
EX: Don't believe him; he is a known liar.
EX: She was caught lying about her age.

wordFormation.meaning: personality: qualities that make a person's character / personal: relating to a particular person rather than anyone else
wordFormation.example:
EX: She has a very outgoing personality.
EX: This is my personal opinion on the matter.

wordFormation.meaning: relative: member of your family / relation: connection between two things / relationship: way in which two people or groups behave towards each other
wordFormation.example:
EX: All my relatives came to the family reunion.
EX: There is a close relation between diet and health.
EX: They have a very strong relationship.

wordPatterns.meaning: liking someone or something very much
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is very fond of her grandchildren.

wordPatterns.meaning: feeling angry or unhappy because someone has something you want
wordPatterns.example: EX: He is jealous of his brother's success.

wordPatterns.meaning: helpful, friendly, and caring towards others
wordPatterns.example: EX: Please be kind to animals.
EX: She is married to a famous writer.

wordPatterns.meaning: feeling pleased and satisfied with something you or someone close to you achieved
wordPatterns.example: EX: Her parents are very proud of her achievements.

wordPatterns.meaning: respect someone for a particular quality or action
wordPatterns.example: EX: I admire him for his honesty.

wordPatterns.meaning: tell someone that you are sorry for something
wordPatterns.example: EX: You should apologise to them for the delay.

wordPatterns.meaning: speak angrily to someone because you disagree
wordPatterns.example: EX: They always argue about money.

wordPatterns.meaning: feel that something is important and worth worrying about
wordPatterns.example: EX: He cares deeply about the environment.

wordPatterns.meaning: talk in a friendly, informal way to someone about something
wordPatterns.example: EX: We chatted about our school days for hours.

wordPatterns.meaning: angry disagreement with someone about a subject
wordPatterns.example: EX: I had a heated argument with my neighbor about the fence.

wordPatterns.meaning: way in which two people or groups behave towards each other
wordPatterns.example: EX: She has a very good working relationship with her boss.
